title: Computer Architecture — RV32IM CPU Labs (Six Stages)
summary: A six-lab series for a Computer Architecture course, each lab implementing a progressively advanced RV32IM CPU microarchitecture in Verilog. Labs 1–4 build in-order 5-stage pipelines with increasing feature sets (exceptions, UART, cache hierarchy), while Labs 5–6 replace the in-order pipeline with out-of-order execution using a scoreboard (Lab 5) and Tomasulo reservation stations with CDB broadcast (Lab 6).
instructionPreview: This project implements a RISC-V (RV32IM) CPU in Verilog across six progressive undergraduate labs, each building a different microarchitectural variant. All labs share common building blocks (ALU, ImmGen, Regs, RAM, comparator, pipeline registers) but differ in pipeline organization, hazard handling, and execution model. You must implement the hollowed-out modules in every lab.
instruction:
This project implements a RISC-V (RV32IM) CPU in Verilog across six progressive undergraduate labs,
each building a different microarchitectural variant. All labs share common building blocks
(ALU, ImmGen, Regs, RAM, comparator, pipeline registers) but differ in pipeline organization,
hazard handling, and execution model. You must implement the hollowed-out modules in every lab.

Your working directory is `/workspace`. The project code is under `/workspace/repo/src/`.

## Lab 1 — Basic 5-Stage In-Order Pipeline (`src/lab1/`)

Classic IF → ID → EX → MEM → WB pipeline for RV32I. The ID stage resolves branches
and contains forwarding muxes.

Modules to implement:
- **ALU** (`core/ALU.v`): 12-operation ALU (ADD, SUB, AND, OR, XOR, SLL, SRL, SRA, SLT,
  SLTU, A+4, B-passthrough) with zero and overflow flags.
- **ImmGen** (`core/ImmGen.v`): Sign-extend immediates for I/B/J/S/U formats.
- **RAM_B** (`core/RAM_B.v`): Byte-addressable data memory with byte/half/word access.
- **Regs** (`core/Regs.v`): 31-entry register file with write-through, dual read ports.
- **REG_IF_ID** (`core/REG_IF_ID.v`): IF/ID latch with stall and flush support.
- **REG_ID_EX** (`core/REG_ID_EX.v`): ID/EX latch with flush-on-hazard support.
- **REG_EX_MEM** (`core/REG_EX_MEM.v`): EX/MEM pipeline register.
- **REG_MEM_WB** (`core/REG_MEM_WB.v`): MEM/WB pipeline register.
- **cmp_32** (`common/cmp_32.v`): 6-mode comparator (EQ, NE, LT, LTU, GE, GEU).
- **CtrlUnit** (`core/CtrlUnit.v`): Full RV32I control signal generation.
- **HazardDetectionUnit** (`core/HazardDetectionUnit.v`): Forwarding and stall logic.
- **RV32core** (`core/RV32core.v`): Top-level MUX wiring (PC select, forwarding, ALU inputs).

## Lab 2 — Pipeline with CSR and Exceptions (`src/lab2/`)

Same 5-stage pipeline as lab1, extended with CSR registers and an exception unit.

Additional modules beyond the lab1 set:
- **CSRRegs** (`core/CSRRegs.v`): CSR register file supporting CSRRW/CSRRS/CSRRC.
- **ExceptionUnit** (`core/ExceptionUnit.v`): Exception handling with PC redirect.
- All lab1-equivalent modules (ALU, CtrlUnit, HazardDetectionUnit, ImmGen, Regs, RAM_B,
  pipeline registers, RV32core) must also be implemented with CSR/exception extensions.

## Lab 3 — Pipeline with UART and Extended Hazards (`src/lab3/`)

5-stage pipeline with UART simulation support in RAM and extended control signals.

Same module set as lab1, plus:
- **RAM_B** includes simulated UART output at address 0x10000000.
- **CtrlUnit** includes CSR read/write signals and exception vector generation.
- **cmp_32** (`common/cmp_32.v`): Same comparator as lab1.

## Lab 4 — Pipeline Variant (`src/lab4/`)

Another 5-stage in-order pipeline variant with a different control signal set.

Same module set as lab1 (ALU, CtrlUnit, HazardDetectionUnit, ImmGen, Regs,
pipeline registers, RV32core). Differences in control signal encoding.

## Lab 5 — Out-of-Order with Scoreboard (`src/lab5/`)

Out-of-order execution using a scoreboard-based controller with dedicated functional units.
Pipeline: IF → Issue → Read Operands → Execute (multiple FUs) → Write-Back.

Modules to implement:
- **FU_ALU** (`core/FU_ALU.v`): Stateful ALU FU — latches inputs on EN, produces result
  in one cycle. Must implement state machine and full ALU computation.
- **FU_mul** (`core/FU_mul.v`): Multiplier FU with 64-cycle latency (shifting state register).
  Instantiate `multiplier` IP, return lower 32 bits.
- **FU_div** (`core/FU_div.v`): Divider FU using AXI handshaking (valid/ready/data).
  Instantiate `divider` IP, return quotient from upper 32 bits.
- **FU_jump** (`core/FU_jump.v`): Jump/branch FU — compare operands with `cmp_32`,
  compute target PC = (JALR ? rs1 : PC) + imm, return address = PC + 4.
- **FU_mem** (`core/FU_mem.v`): Memory FU with 2-cycle latency. Compute address via
  `add_32`, instantiate `RAM_B` for memory access.

## Lab 6 — Out-of-Order with Tomasulo/Reservation Stations (`src/lab6/`)

Full Tomasulo OoO execution with reservation stations, CDB broadcast, and RAT.

Modules to implement:
- **FU_ALU/FU_mul/FU_div/FU_jump/FU_mem**: Same functional units as lab5.
- **CtrlUnit** (`core/CtrlUnit.v`): Instruction decode with FU routing and op encoding.
- **Regs** (`core/Regs.v`): Register file with RAT (Register Alias Table) for Tomasulo.
  Implement `rs_num_A`/`rs_num_B` RAT lookups and CDB broadcast writeback.
- **RAM_B** (`core/RAM_B.v`): Extended RAM with UART simulation and larger address space.
- **ImmGen** (`core/ImmGen.v`): Immediate generator (same as other labs).
- **RV32core** (`core/RV32core.v`): Top-level wiring for Tomasulo — PC enable, issue enable,
  RS allocation, reservation station port connections, FU port connections, CDB broadcast,
  and stall logic.

## Test-Coupled Signatures

The test suite compiles Verilog sources with iverilog and runs simulation via a test program
pre-loaded into instruction ROM. Tests verify register write-back values at specific PCs.
The testbench monitors WB-stage signals via hierarchical access to the core module.

moduleDag.project: Computer Architecture — RV32IM CPU Labs (Six Stages)
moduleDag.description: A six-lab series for a Computer Architecture course, each lab implementing a progressively advanced RV32IM CPU microarchitecture in Verilog. Labs 1–4 build in-order 5-stage pipelines with increasing feature sets (exceptions, UART, cache hierarchy), while Labs 5–6 replace the in-order pipeline with out-of-order execution using a scoreboard (Lab 5) and Tomasulo reservation stations with CDB broadcast (Lab 6).

nodes.label: Lab 1 — Basic 5-Stage In-Order Pipeline
nodes.description: Classic IF→ID→EX→MEM→WB RV32I pipeline. The ID stage resolves branches and contains forwarding muxes. Implements: 12-operation ALU, 5-format immediate generator, byte-addressable data RAM, 31-entry register file with write-through, four pipeline latches (IF/ID, ID/EX, EX/MEM, MEM/WB) with stall/flush support, a 6-mode comparator, full RV32I control unit, hazard detection unit generating forwarding selects and stall/flush signals, and the top-level RV32core wiring all submodules together.

nodes.label: Lab 2 — Pipeline with CSR and Exception Handling
nodes.description: Extends the Lab 1 5-stage pipeline with M-mode CSR registers and a dedicated ExceptionUnit. CSRRegs implements CSRRW/CSRRS/CSRRC. ExceptionUnit monitors instruction-level faults and triggers a PC redirect that flushes all in-flight stages. All lab1 modules are re-implemented with CSR/exception signal extensions; EX/MEM latch carries CSR and exception signals; RV32core adds the exception redirect mux and cancel signals for suppressing register and memory writes on faulted instructions.

nodes.label: Lab 3 — Pipeline with UART Simulation and Extended Hazards
nodes.description: A standalone 5-stage RV32I pipeline variant built on the same module set as Lab 1. RAM_B adds simulated UART output: word stores to address 0x10000000 print the ASCII character of the lower byte. CtrlUnit emits CSR read/write control signals and a 2-bit exception vector. HazardDetectionUnit is extended to handle the broader control signal set. All modules (ALU, ImmGen, Regs, pipeline registers, CtrlUnit, HazardDetectionUnit, cmp_32, RV32core) are re-implemented independently from lab1 and lab2.

nodes.label: Lab 4 — In-Order Pipeline Variant with Cache Hierarchy
nodes.description: A 5-stage RV32I pipeline variant that replaces the direct RAM_B connection with a two-level memory hierarchy: a Cache Management Unit (CMU, pre-provided) sits between the MEM stage and a separate data_ram module. The CMU introduces a cmu_stall signal that HazardDetectionUnit must propagate to freeze the pipeline on cache misses. All core pipeline modules (ALU, ImmGen, Regs, pipeline latches, CtrlUnit, HazardDetectionUnit, RV32core) use a different control signal encoding than other labs.

nodes.label: Lab 5 — Out-of-Order Execution with Scoreboard
nodes.description: Replaces the in-order pipeline with a scoreboard-based out-of-order execution engine. Pipeline stages: IF → Issue → Read Operands → Execute (parallel FUs) → Write-Back. Five dedicated functional units are implemented: FU_ALU (1-cycle stateful ALU), FU_mul (64-cycle multiplier IP with shift-register state), FU_div (AXI handshake with divider IP), FU_jump (branch/jump target and return address), FU_mem (2-cycle memory access via add_32 and RAM_B). The scoreboard, CtrlUnit, RV32core, Regs, and ImmGen are pre-provided; only the five FU modules require implementation.

nodes.label: Lab 6 — Out-of-Order Execution with Tomasulo Algorithm (Bonus)
nodes.description: Full Tomasulo out-of-order execution with reservation stations (RS), Common Data Bus (CDB) broadcast, and a Register Alias Table (RAT) integrated into the register file. Regs implements the RAT array (rs_num_A/rs_num_B tag lookups) and CDB broadcast writeback. CtrlUnit is extended with FU routing signals. The five FU modules share the same interface as Lab 5. RV32core wires up RS modules per FU, manages PC/issue stall logic, selects the CDB source among finishing FUs, and drives wb_addr/wb_data for test monitors. Lab6 verification also re-runs Lab 3's test program (lab3_rom.mem/lab3_ram.mem).

edges.label: lab2 extends lab1's 5-stage pipeline with CSR registers and exception handling; all lab1 modules reappear with CSR/exception signal additions
lab3 reimplements the same lab1 module set (ALU, ImmGen, Regs, cmp_32, pipeline registers, CtrlUnit, HazardDetectionUnit, RV32core) with UART and extended control signals
lab4 reimplements the same lab1 pipeline module set with a different control signal encoding and a CMU cache hierarchy
lab5 reuses the same ALU computation model, ImmGen formats, and Regs primitives established in lab1; prior pipeline understanding is prerequisite for the OoO FU design
lab6's RAM_B extends lab3's UART-simulation design, and lab6 verification re-runs lab3's test program (lab3_rom.mem/lab3_ram.mem) requiring lab3's pipeline concepts
lab6 reuses the same five FU module interfaces (FU_ALU, FU_mul, FU_div, FU_jump, FU_mem) from lab5, plugging them into a Tomasulo reservation-station framework
